regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a multi-front crisis spanning April-June 2026: a failed ceasefire punctuated by US naval blockade enforcement (Touska seizure on April 19), Iranian retaliation via drone strikes, escalating Hormuz control claims, comprehensive Israeli strikes on June 14-15 eliminating IRGC leadership and energy infrastructure, cascading internal instability with mandatory Basij deployment, succession upheaval from March 3 Assembly strike, economic freefall, and weakened regional proxy networks following Assad's fall.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 14-15 killed IRGC Intelligence Chief Mohammad Kazemi and deputies while destroying critical energy infrastructure including Shahran oil depot, regime ministries, and airbases, simultaneously degrading coercive security apparatus capacity and forcing emergency Basij nationwide deployment to suppress dissent.
